content:
9 min read
Just now

--

I paid someone to hack my code.
Not a black hat on the dark web. A security researcher. White hat. Contracted. Fully above board. I gave them two identical APIs. One written in Python with FastAPI. One written in Rust with Axum. Same endpoints. Same logic. Same database.
The mission was simple. Find vulnerabilities. Exploit them. Show me what breaks and how badly.
I expected Rust to be a little better. Maybe fewer crashes. Maybe cleaner error handling. What I did not expect was the Python API falling over in 12 minutes while the Rust API sat there like a concrete bunker for three days straight.
The difference was not about frameworks. It was not about syntax. It was about memory. And that difference is the scariest thing I have seen in production.
Why I Even Did This
I have been writing Python for eight years. I have shipped apps to production. I have handled user data. I have processed payments. And I have always told myself the same comforting lie.
“Python is safe enough. The interpreter handles memory. I do not manage pointers. I cannot have buffer overflows. I am fine.”
Then I read the statistics. Microsoft says 70% of their security vulnerabilities are memory safety issues. Google says…
